body.label: func_metric_11.root.thm
body.claim: Let $X = \mathbb{R}$ and define $d: \mathbb{R} \times \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ by $d(x,y)=|x^3-y^3|$. Then $d$ is a metric on $\mathbb{R}$.

proof_steps.claim: Let $X = \mathbb{R}$ and define $d: \mathbb{R} \times \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ by $d(x,y)=|x^3-y^3|$. Then $d$ is a metric on $\mathbb{R}$.
